2018-07-25.title: Palestinians to Lead U.N.’s Biggest Bloc of Developing Countries
2018-07-25.abstract: The largest bloc of developing countries at the United Nations has chosen Palestine, an observer state, as its next leader.

2018-07-25.title: The Bread Thief
2018-07-25.abstract: “The only thing stopping a bad guy with a foot-long breadstick is a good guy with his own foot-long breadstick!”

2018-07-25.title: Fact-Checking Pompeo on Iran
2018-07-25.abstract: The secretary of state, in a speech to Iranian-Americans, sought their support in an indictment of Iran’s leaders, whom he likened to Mafia gangsters.

2018-07-26.title: Toronto Shooting Rekindles Familiar Debate: Terrorist? Mentally Ill? Both?
2018-07-26.abstract: The family of the gunman who shot 15 people says he had serious psychiatric problems. The Islamic State claimed him as one of its own.

2018-07-26.title: Australian Media Merger Stirs Fear of ‘Catastrophic’ Deal
2018-07-26.abstract: Fairfax Media and Nine announced plans to merge under the Nine name, which would make it one of Australia’s largest media groups.

2018-07-26.title: From Manus to Trump’s America
2018-07-26.abstract: I first met Imran Mohammad in one of Australia’s offshore detention camps. Now he’s in the United States. Will trauma or hope prevail?

2018-07-26.title: Japan Executes 6 Members of Cult Behind Sarin Attack
2018-07-26.abstract: The execution of the six came nearly three weeks after the group’s leader, Shoko Asahara, was put to death along with six other followers.

2018-07-26.title: U.S. Embassy Street in Beijing Is Rocked by Blast
2018-07-26.abstract: The explosion occurred on Thursday afternoon outside the building in an area where Chinese citizens line up each day to apply for visas.

2018-07-27.title: United Nations Leader Warns of a Cash Shortage
2018-07-27.abstract: Secretary General António Guterres said “delayed contributions” had caused a cash shortfall “unlike those we have experienced previously.”

2018-07-27.title: Remains of 55 U.S. War Dead in North Korea Start Journey Home After 65 Years
2018-07-27.abstract: President Trump thanked North Korea’s leader, Kim Jong-un, for the return of the remains believed to be American servicemen killed during the Korean War.

2018-07-27.title: Delay in Sydney Jail Death Inquest Prolongs Family’s ‘Agonizing Wait’
2018-07-27.abstract: A hearing on the death of David Dungay Jr., an Indigenous Australian who died after being pinned by correctional officers, has been adjourned until March.

2018-07-27.title: The Last Days of a Hong Kong Street for Singing Your Heart Out
2018-07-27.abstract: A pedestrian zone in the Mong Kok neighborhood of Hong Kong is closing after criticism that street performers have made it too loud.

2018-07-27.title: Putin Invites Trump to Moscow for Second Meeting After Washington Postponed Plans
2018-07-27.abstract: The Russian president continued to push for a second meeting, saying he was also ready to meet in Washington.

2018-07-28.title: Prime Minister’s Eating Habits Scandalize Australians: It’s Piegate
2018-07-28.abstract: Prime Minister Malcolm Turnbull’s approach to eating a meat pie set off a debate over what was seen by some as a food faux pas.

2018-07-28.title: Go Home, South Korea Tells Workers, as Stress Takes Its Toll
2018-07-28.abstract: Though Japan brought the world the concept of “death from overwork,” South Koreans actually work longer hours. The government is trying to change that.

2018-07-28.title: Facebook Is Failing to Aid Inquiry Into ‘Fake News,’ British Lawmakers Say
2018-07-28.abstract: In a report to be released Sunday, a parliamentary committee accused Facebook of providing “disingenuous” answers to questions and of withholding information.

2018-07-28.title: From a Space Station in Argentina, China Expands Its Reach in Latin America
2018-07-28.abstract: Our correspondent went to the deserts of Patagonia to examine how China secured its new base, a symbol of its growing clout in the region.

2018-07-28.title: Extremists Kill 2 at Afghan School for Midwives, but Students Escape
2018-07-28.abstract: Security forces battled insurgents at a midwifery school in Jalalabad, and two attackers died. Three female students were wounded.

2018-07-29.title: Cambodia Re-Elects Its Leader, a Result Predetermined by One
2018-07-29.abstract: Hun Sen, the longtime prime minister, worked hard to control the election, even as he flouts the rules of democracy.

2018-07-29.title: Pet Cremation: Seeking ‘Good Afterlife’ for Bangkok’s Pampered Dogs
2018-07-29.abstract: Thailand’s capital, with little green space and deadly reptiles, is not ideal for pets. But owners spoil their dogs and cats in life — and often cremate them in a Buddhist ceremony when they die.

2018-07-29.title: On Spain’s Smartest Streets, a Property Boom Made in Venezuela
2018-07-29.abstract: Madrid is attracting thousands of rich Venezuelans who have been fleeing their country’s political and economic turmoil.

2018-07-29.title: Laos Dam Failure Exposes Cracks in a Secretive Government’s Agenda
2018-07-29.abstract: The deadly accident highlights a policy of selling resources to foreign companies while ignoring the dangers to the rural poor, analysts say.

2018-07-29.title: Ahed Tamimi, Palestinian Teenager Who Slapped Israeli Soldier, Is Released From Jail
2018-07-29.abstract: The 17-year-old received a hero’s welcome, was greeted by the Palestinian president and says she now wants to fight Israeli occupation by studying law.

2018-07-30.title: 11 and Married: Malaysia Spars Over an Age-Old Practice
2018-07-30.abstract: An 11-year-old girl’s marriage to a 41-year-old man — the father of her best friend — has reignited debate in this modern multiethnic democracy about the persistence of weddings for girls under 18.

2018-07-30.title: Where Churches Have Become Temples of Cheese, Fitness and Eroticism
2018-07-30.abstract: Dozens of churches in Quebec have been repurposed into reading rooms, luxury condos, cheese emporiums and upmarket fitness centers in a Canadian province where the Catholic Church is in decline.

2018-07-30.title: Drought Relief Is Coming. Australia’s Farmers Say It’s Nowhere Near Enough.
2018-07-30.abstract: Farmers in Australia, where a drought is drying out an area larger than Texas, are demanding more help than the government is offering.

2018-07-30.title: No Plane. No Remains. And Now, No Real Answers on Malaysia Airlines Flight 370.
2018-07-30.abstract: Four years after the flight bound for Beijing vanished, a 495-page official report rebutted some conspiracy theories but provided no definitive conclusions.

2018-07-30.title: In Myanmar, Flood Warnings Come After the Floods
2018-07-30.abstract: This year’s monsoon season has brought crippling floods to Southeast Asia, in some cases worsened by a lack of emergency preparations by governments.

2018-07-31.title: Ceramics Aren’t Enough. Bring on the Spaceships, Italian Town Says.
2018-07-31.abstract: Italy decided that the ceramics center of Grottaglie — with its long runway and uneventful weather — had the right stuff to be Virgin Galactic’s next launchpad.

2018-07-31.title: Philippine Bombing Kills 10, Showing Insurgents Remain a Problem
2018-07-31.abstract: Officials say the blast was carried out by Abu Sayyaf, a militant group that besieged the southern city of Marawi for five months.

2018-07-31.title: In Cambodia, Dissenting Voters Find Ways to Say ‘None of the Above’
2018-07-31.abstract: The authoritarian prime minister Hun Sen’s party won the election Sunday. But the second-largest number of votes went to no one at all.

2018-07-31.title: Afghan Army Takes Over After ISIS Attacks a Refugee Office
2018-07-31.abstract: Islamic State extremists have struck civilians in Jalalabad at least 10 times this year, in a series of suicide attacks against lightly defended places.

2018-07-31.title: Erdogan’s Most Charismatic Rival in Turkey Challenges Him, From Jail
2018-07-31.abstract: Despite spending more than 20 months in prison, the Kurdish political leader Selahattin Demirtas finished third in Turkey’s election. But life may only get harder now.
